method: HENDRIX-Φ structured search operationalization via M32 + Yang-Mills curvature gating + phi gradient proposals

interpretation.phi_improvement: HENDRIX-Φ mean phi_resonance is +2.84% above linear scan.
interpretation.top_candidates: HENDRIX-Φ top-10% phi_resonance is +2.42% above linear scan — meaning its best nonce candidates are stronger search targets.
interpretation.domain_coverage: HENDRIX-Φ visits 32/32 M32 domains (+0 vs linear scan fewer).
interpretation.yang_mills_gating: HENDRIX-Φ mass gate pass rate: 95.9% (linear: 100.0%). Lower gate pass rate means the search is concentrating on low-curvature (mass-gapped) nonces.
interpretation.verdict: PARTIAL: HENDRIX-Φ shows measurable improvement in some metrics but not all. The structural search is finding better nonces, but the effect is modest at this sample size.
